title: Touch 4: Transcript-to-Proposal Pipeline
description: Walk through the complete Touch 4 transcript-to-proposal pipeline: paste a meeting transcript, watch AtlusDeck extract fields, review drafts of all 3 artifacts (proposal deck, talk track, FAQ), request changes, and receive finalized Google Drive links.

steps.narration: Welcome to Touch 4 -- the most comprehensive touch in AtlusDeck. From a single meeting transcript, you'll get three polished artifacts: a proposal deck, a talk track, and a buyer FAQ. Let's start on the Touch 4 page for the Meridian Dynamics deal.
Here's the transcript input area. This is where you paste the notes from your meeting -- AtlusDeck will extract everything it needs from the raw text.

After pasting in your transcript, the notes are ready for processing. You can already see concrete deal details like budget, pilot timing, integration requirements, and brand consistency goals in the text box.

actions.value:
Meeting with Meridian Dynamics VP of Sales and CTO on March 12, 2026.

Attendees: Rachel Torres (VP Sales), David Kim (CTO), James Liu (Head of Operations)

Key Discussion Points:
- Current proposal turnaround is 5-7 business days, losing deals to faster competitors
- Need to reduce proposal generation to under 2 hours without sacrificing quality
- Budget approved for Q2 rollout at $180K annual, with potential expansion to $350K for enterprise tier
- Must integrate with existing Google Workspace environment (Slides, Docs, Drive)
- SOC 2 compliance documentation required before procurement can sign off
- Pilot starting April 2026, full rollout target by end of Q2 2026
- Want to improve win rate by 15% through personalized, data-driven proposals
- Need to scale proposal capacity without adding headcount
- CTO wants API access for custom integrations with their CRM
- VP Sales emphasized brand consistency across all generated materials

steps.narration: Click Submit to kick off the extraction process. AtlusDeck is now analyzing your transcript to pull out structured fields -- customer context, outcomes, constraints, stakeholders, timeline, and budget.

The AI has extracted structured fields from your transcript. Take a moment to review everything -- customer context, business outcomes, constraints, stakeholders, timeline, and budget are all pulled directly from what you pasted.

Notice how the transcript has been organized into clear review fields. AtlusDeck breaks the notes into customer context, business outcomes, constraints, stakeholders, timeline, and budget so you can validate each section quickly.

Approve the extracted fields and AtlusDeck generates a draft sales brief. This is the review stage where you check the executive summary, solution pillars, and ROI framing before the final assets are assembled.
Here's the sales brief draft. The AI has framed the solution around intelligent proposal generation, human-in-the-loop quality assurance, and Google Workspace integration for Meridian Dynamics.
If the draft needs work, send it back for another pass. AtlusDeck regenerates the brief with your feedback so the next version is tighter before you move on to final asset generation.
The refined brief is back. Notice the stronger executive summary, sharper ROI framing, and clearer solution pillars tailored to Meridian Dynamics.
Approve the refined brief and AtlusDeck produces the final proposal assets. Now you can review the proposal, talk track, and FAQ from their own tabs before marking everything ready.
The Proposal tab links out to the final deck in Google Slides. This is the presentation asset generated from the transcript and the approved sales brief.
Switch to Talk Track and you get the companion Google Doc for speaking notes and delivery guidance.
The FAQ tab gives you a separate Google Doc for buyer questions covering pricing, timeline, integration, compliance, and scale.
Give the final approval and all three artifacts are saved to Google Drive. The entire workflow -- from raw transcript to three polished, brand-compliant documents -- took minutes instead of days.
Here are the saved artifacts together -- proposal deck, talk track, and buyer FAQ -- with direct Drive access from this completed state.
